sections.paragraph:
(“gift”), [Beriah], pr.n.-

(1) of a son of Ephraim; 1Ch 7:23, “and he called his name Beriah, כִּי בְרָעָה הָיְתָה בְבֵיתֹו because there was a gift to his house.” So indeed Michaëlis, Suppl. 224, but apparently more correctly, “because there was a calamity to his house,” בְּרָעָה for רָעָה with Beth essentiœ, as it is called (p. 99, A ); compare verses 22 1Ch 7:22, 23 1Ch 7:23LXX. ὅτι ἐν κακοῖς ἐγένετο ἐν οἴκῳ μου, Vulg. eo quod in malis domus ejus ortus esset.-(2) m., Gen 46:17.-(3) 1Ch 8:13.-(4) 1Ch 23:10. From No. 2 is patron.-
